mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as corporate revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS is increasing, or Rank IC is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 2.5 percent, restrictive monetary policy increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on FEDFUNDS > 2.5% is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) captures broad-based demand expansion across consumer and residential investment channels, with their interaction amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are not higher when both series rise together versus when only one rises.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market momentum and impending earnings compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires during late-cycle periods, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects manufacturing sector health and capacity utilization improvements, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as production surprises drive earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of weeks when INDPRO is rising, or the information coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising more than 0.5 percent over two months erodes real corporate margins and raises the probability of tighter Federal Reserve policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered inflation-acceleration episodes, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) surprises relative to trend signal aggregate demand strength that supports top-line revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as macro expectations are repriced.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 is expanding, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 changes and forward returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated unemployment (UNRATE above 5.0 percent) and restrictive Fed funds rates (FEDFUNDS above 2.5 percent) captures stagflationary risk where growth slows while policy remains tight, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year window, or positive 5-day returns occur in more than 45% of jointly triggered instances.
